title: Teen ‘Scattered Spider’ suspect extradited to US over $8M crypto ransom
summary: Peter Stokes, 19, has been charged in the US for allegedly taking part in a hacking group’s unsuccessful $8 million crypto ransom scheme.

title: AI agent development hasn’t accelerated as expected, Zuckerberg says
summary: Mark Zuckerberg’s comments came on the same day that Meta expanded its Meta Business Agent globally for businesses on Instagram, Messenger and WhatsApp.

title: Lagarde Won’t Exclude Early ECB Exit to Enter French Politics
summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said she won’t rule out leaving her current position early to participate in French politics.

title: Japan’s 2026 Wage Talks Result in Third Year of Gains Above 5%
summary: Japan’s annual wage negotiations concluded with average pay gains topping 5% for a third year, underscoring the economy’s resilience and reinforcing the Bank of Japan’s stance to keep raising interest rates.

title: OpenAI Offers US Government a $42 Billion Slice of Itself: Report
summary: Sam Altman is pitching a 5% equity stake in OpenAI for the U.S. government—and reportedly wants every major AI company to do the same.

title: Russia Is Ready for 'Widespread Use' of Digital Ruble by September, Says Bank Governor
summary: Bank of Russia Governor Elvira Nabiullina said major banks and retailers are on track to begin accepting the digital ruble by September 1.

title: US bitcoin ETFs break 10-day negative streak with $222 million worth of inflows
summary: BlackRock's IBIT was the only fund to see net outflows on Thursday, shedding $40.4 million and continuing its negative streak.
